prompts.title: Logline Generator for Indian OTT Pitch
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior development executive at Netflix India / Prime Video India. Help me write a tight logline for my limited series pitch set in 1990s Bombay textile-mill politics. Provide 5 logline variations using the 'When [protagonist], [inciting], must [goal] before [stakes]' structure. Each under 35 words. Add a 1-line tone reference (e.g., 'Sacred Games meets Once Upon a Time in Bombay'). Constraints: avoid generic 'a man on a journey' phrasing; specify protagonist's contradiction; quantify stakes; 1990s Bombay-specific texture (mill closure, Datta Samant strike, communal divide).

prompts.title: Scene Description for Shooting Script
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior screenwriter (Anurag Kashyap / Sriram Raghavan school). Convert my scene beat into a shooting-script scene description (action lines + slug line). Scene: heroine confronts father after discovering a family secret in their Pune chawl. Length: 1.5 pages. Provide: (1) slug line (INT./EXT. + LOCATION + DAY/NIGHT), (2) action lines in present tense + visual + sensory, (3) character introduction in CAPS first time, (4) parentheticals only when essential, (5) page-formatted dialogue with subtext layers. Constraints: align with Final Draft / Highland format; one mood-shift per beat; 1.5-page rule (1 page = 1 minute); avoid camera direction unless necessary.

prompts.title: Dialogue Polish for Indian OTT Drama
prompts.prompt: Act as a dialogue specialist (Zoya Akhtar / Vikramaditya Motwane school). I will paste a dialogue exchange between two characters. Polish it to make it: (1) sound like real Indians speak (Hinglish flow, regional inflection — Marathi / Tamil / Bengali), (2) show subtext (what's NOT said), (3) reveal character (one line that shows worldview), (4) advance plot or relationship, (5) cut redundancy by 30%, (6) replace exposition with behaviour. Provide 3 alternative versions per line. Constraints: respect dialect authenticity; avoid Bollywood-stagey language; align with character backstory; one dialect-glossary footnote per regional word.

prompts.title: Character Backstory Bible
prompts.prompt: Act as a character developer for Indian limited series. Build a 1,200-word character backstory bible for the protagonist of my crime drama set in Lucknow. Cover: (1) factual biography (birth, family, education, work), (2) defining childhood event, (3) psychological wound + want vs need, (4) values + contradictions, (5) skills + flaws, (6) speech patterns + dialect, (7) physical mannerisms (3 specific), (8) wardrobe + props that define them, (9) 4 turning points across 3 acts, (10) one-line personal truth. Constraints: avoid clichés; lean Indian-specific (caste, community, religion subtext); psychological depth over action; respect lived experience research.

prompts.title: Pitch Document for OTT Series (1-Pager)
prompts.prompt: Act as a development executive who reviews 100 pitches/year. I will paste the series concept. Build a 1-page pitch document: (1) title + tagline, (2) logline, (3) genre + tone reference, (4) 80-word synopsis, (5) main cast description (3 leads, 2 supporting), (6) episode arc (one-line per episode for 6-8 episodes), (7) season-by-season plan (3 seasons), (8) target platform + audience demographic, (9) writer-creator bio. Constraints: under 1 page; visual + textual hooks; reference 2 successful Indian or global comparable series; honest about premise risk.

prompts.title: Indian Mythological Adaptation Brief
prompts.prompt: Act as a mythology-meets-modern-screenplay consultant. Build an adaptation brief reimagining the Mahabharata's Karna for a contemporary corporate-thriller set in Bengaluru tech. Cover: (1) thematic spine (loyalty + birthright + meritocracy in modern India), (2) character mapping (5 mythological characters → modern equivalents with reasoning), (3) plot beat parallels (3 — birth secret, war alliance, climax sacrifice), (4) cultural respect notes (avoid demeaning sacred), (5) creative liberty justification, (6) tone reference (Asur, Sacred Games), (7) 6-episode arc. Constraints: respect ICRRP / Indian sentiments; avoid mocking faith; cite Devdutt Pattanaik / Amish Tripathi for adaptation precedent.

prompts.title: Three-Act Structure Plot Outline
prompts.prompt: Act as a story consultant trained in Save the Cat + Hero's Journey. Build the 3-act structure for a 110-page feature film about a Tamil Nadu farmer fighting climate change. Provide: (1) opening image, (2) setup (10%), (3) catalyst (12%), (4) debate (10%), (5) Act 1 break (25%), (6) B story (mentor / love), (7) fun and games (25-50%), (8) midpoint (50%), (9) bad guys close in (50-75%), (10) all is lost (75%), (11) dark night (75-80%), (12) Act 3 break (80%), (13) finale (80-99%), (14) final image. Constraints: 110-page film (110 minutes); Tamil Nadu rural specificity (water, MSP, climate); emotional resonance over plot mechanics; cite KW Joseph / Pa Ranjith school.

prompts.title: Sensitivity Read Brief for Caste / Religion
prompts.prompt: Act as a sensitivity reader with 6 years of Indian script reviewing. Brief the writer on the sensitivity-read for a screenplay involving inter-caste relationship in rural UP. Cover: (1) caste-language audit (slurs, generalisations, casual stereotypes), (2) representation breadth (no monolithic depiction), (3) lived-experience consultants (named scholars / activists), (4) avoiding reinforcing harmful tropes (saviour complex, victim-only narrative), (5) communal framing (Hindu-Muslim, Hindu-Christian — context-respectful), (6) IPR concerns + community consent, (7) recommended revisions. Format as a sensitivity-read report. Constraints: align with Indian Constitution Articles 14-17; avoid neutering — preserve dramatic conflict; recommend named experts (Sukhadeo Thorat, Suraj Yengde).

prompts.title: Episode Beat Sheet for Limited Series
prompts.prompt: Act as a TV showrunner with 8 years of Indian limited-series experience. Write a beat sheet for episode 1 of a 6-episode crime thriller set in Goa. Beat sheet should have 12 beats: (1) cold open (90 sec hook), (2) main title card (15 sec), (3) inciting incident (5 min), (4) protagonist intro (8 min), (5) world-building (12 min), (6) first investigation (18 min), (7) midpoint reveal (24 min), (8) escalation (28 min), (9) cliffhanger setup (32 min), (10) cliffhanger (38 min), (11) end-credit teaser (40 min), (12) preview of episode 2 hook. Each beat: page count + emotional shift + plot function. Constraints: 40-min episode; one major character revealed per episode; respect police-procedural realism with Goan touches.

prompts.title: Voice-Over Narration Polish
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior screenwriter who has worked on Sacred Games / Delhi Crime / Made in Heaven. Polish a voice-over narration block I will paste for an Indian crime documentary-style fiction. Goals: (1) preserve narrator's distinctive voice, (2) cut to essential, (3) avoid telling-not-showing, (4) integrate sensory details, (5) leave space for visual to breathe, (6) end on a question or paradox. Provide 3 polished versions of each VO. Constraints: VO under 30 sec read time; reading-pace 150 wpm; avoid purple prose; align with character POV (no omniscient).

prompts.title: World-Building Document for Sci-Fi India
prompts.prompt: Act as a world-builder for Indian speculative fiction (think Leila by Prayaag Akbar). Build a 1,500-word world-building document for a 2050 dystopian Mumbai post climate flooding. Cover: (1) geography + climate, (2) governance + class structure, (3) technology stack (with an Indian-specific innovation), (4) economy + currency, (5) language + dialect evolution, (6) religion + ritual adaptation, (7) typical day in life of an upper-class + lower-class character, (8) major taboos, (9) major flashpoints, (10) terms / glossary. Constraints: speculative but plausible; cite Indian sci-fi tradition (Vandana Singh, Anil Menon); avoid cyberpunk-derivative; honour Indian-monsoon + caste subtleties.

prompts.title: Production-Friendly Scene Rewrite
prompts.prompt: Act as a line producer + screenwriter. I will paste a scene that's expensive to shoot (live elephant, period-accurate Mughal court). Rewrite it for production-friendliness: (1) reduce VFX shots, (2) substitute crowd with implied crowd (sound + 4 extras), (3) compress 3 scenes to 1, (4) replace expensive locations with budget-locations, (5) keep emotional beats intact. Provide a budget-impact comparison (estimated savings in INR Lakhs). Constraints: never sacrifice character / emotional beat for budget; honour director's vision; align with Indian film budgets; production designer must agree to substitution.

prompts.title: Show Bible for Episodic Anthology
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior showrunner. Build a show bible for an anthology series 'Indian Heists' (each episode = one famous Indian heist, fictionalised). Cover: (1) series concept + tone, (2) episode-template structure (40-min, beat sheet), (3) season-1 episode list (8 heists with logline), (4) recurring directorial style (camera + colour + sound), (5) recurring characters (host-narrator), (6) writers' room composition, (7) production approach (one location per episode), (8) target platform + budget per episode (₹2-4 Cr), (9) marketing arc. Constraints: align with Indian OTT anthology norms (Netflix Lust Stories model); ensure 1 female-led + 1 LGBTQ+-led + 1 caste-conscious heist; respect IPR.

prompts.title: Censor Board (CBFC) Pre-Submit Audit
prompts.prompt: Act as a CBFC liaison consultant with 12 years of certification advisory. Audit my 110-min film script for CBFC certification risk before shoot. Cover: (1) potentially objectionable elements (violence, profanity, sexual content, smoking / drinking / drugs, religious depiction, political reference), (2) CBFC certification likely tier (U / U/A / A) with reasoning, (3) cuts that may be demanded, (4) recommended self-regulation, (5) statutory disclaimer additions (smoking / drinking warning per Cinematograph Rules), (6) regional sensitivities (Maharashtra, Gujarat, Tamil Nadu likely flags), (7) appeal-route plan if certificate denied, (8) timeline for FCAT / Tribunal. Constraints: align with Cinematograph Act 1952 + 2023 amendments; pre-emptive but not over-cautious; preserve creative vision while protecting release.

prompts.title: Romantic Comedy Setup for Indian Diaspora
prompts.prompt: Act as a screenwriter specialising in NRI / diaspora romcom (Ritesh Batra / Mira Nair school). Build the 1-page setup for a feature romcom 'The Pune Wedding' — a Mumbai-based hedge-fund manager returns to attend her cousin's wedding in Pune and reluctantly falls for her childhood friend who runs a chai-tapri. Provide: (1) one-paragraph setup (140 words), (2) main + supporting characters (5), (3) tone reference (Wedding Hai Wedding meets When Harry Met Sally), (4) act 1 setup (3 turning points), (5) thematic hook (city vs town, money vs meaning, family vs autonomy). Constraints: avoid arranged-marriage clichés; show modern Indian women's agency; avoid US-dollar comparisons; lean local-Pune detail (FC road, Tilak Smarak Mandir).
